name: Botkeeper
tagline: AI-powered automated bookkeeping for accounting firms
description: Botkeeper raised $90M for AI bookkeeping and claimed 98% accuracy — then shut down in February 2026, killed by macroeconomic shifts affecting its largest clients.
story: Founded in 2015, Botkeeper raised approximately $90 million — including a $42 million Series C in 2021 — to automate bookkeeping for accounting firms using AI. The irony of Botkeeper's demise is almost poetic: the founder claimed their AI product had finally become 'the AI powerhouse we always dreamed of' with 98% accuracy just before announcing the shutdown. But technical excellence couldn't save a business model undermined by macroeconomic shifts and industry consolidation that gutted its largest clients' revenues. In February 2026, Botkeeper closed its doors, proving that even a well-funded AI startup with a working product can fail when the market shifts beneath it.
